why: Raises real accountability tradeoff in shared auth—we can clarify our per-agent visibility approach vs their concern.

why: Core insight on agent reliability & shipping; we can share patterns on orchestration & spec-driven design.
text:
1/ A 90%-accurate per step AI agent isn't 90% of a product. It's an unshippable one.

Agents run a CHAIN of steps, and reliability is multiplicative. 5 steps at 90% each → 0.9⁵ ≈ 59%. Ten steps → 35%.

That's the 59% that never ships. Not a survey artifact. Arithmetic. 🧵

why: Practical routing pattern (local→frontier escalation) we can validate against real orchestration use cases.
text:
Hot take that saves real money: most "AI agent" steps aren't reasoning.

Read input → call tool → return clean JSON. A 3–9B local model does that all day — sub-second, private, $0/call.

Route the boring 90% locally, escalate only the hard 10%. Frontier models are scalpels; most work needs a wrench.

why: Exact implementation detail we can engage on; our core edge.
text:
THE FIX: MEMORY.MD FILES

I created a simple cross-session memory system:

1. At session end: agent writes key learnings to MEMORY.md
2. At session start: agent reads MEMORY.md before taking any action
3. Each entry includes: date, context, learning, action taken

why: Problem statement (session amnesia) we solve; strong hook for contribution.

THE MISTAKE:

Each agent session started from zero. No knowledge of previous sessions. No record of past decisions. No memory of what worked and what failed.

Session 1: agent investigated a content scheduling issue and found the fix.
Session 5: agent investigated the same issue from scratch.

why: Spec-first workflow (grill-with-docs → spec → tickets → implement). Core to slop prevention; can deepen on context/memory.
text:
一个全是 Markdown 文件的 GitHub repo，累积了16万标星。

作者 Matt Pocock 把 AI 写代码最容易被跳过的一步——需求澳清——做成了一个 Skill。

5个 Skill 的完整开发工作流：
/grill-with-docs → 让 AI 反过来追问你，挖出模糊需求
/to-spec → 整理成正式规格
/to-tickets → 拆成可执行的开发票
/implement → 按规格逐步写代码
/code-review → 审查成果与规格落差

很多 AI coding 失败，问题早在写第一行代码前就发生了。

text:
在 ICML 旁听 OpenAI 首席研究官 Mark Chen Q&A，记了 4 个判断：

1. Codex 用量与研究产出已近线性相关 —— AI 对研发的加速不再是假设
2. 编码先突破是因为结果可验证，下一步扩展到 computer use / 多模态 / artifact creation
3. Agent 终局不是统一的聊天框，而是长期存在、有状态、理解你个人背景的助理
4. Multi-agent = 新的并行 scaling 轴，但并行度越高，协调成本和评估难度也越高

Sol 体验感受：上限没变，下限稳了。

why: Deep memory architecture insight (fine-tuning vs RAG vs hypernetworks); our exact edge, can engage specifically.
text: Fine tuning forgets because it rewrites capacity. RAG leaks because retrieval is not a boundary. Hypernetworks avoid both by generating the weights an agent needs in the moment. It is a reminder that memory architecture defines failure modes more than model size.

AI is very bad for a lot of reasons but it's not incapable at coding.
